info.description:
Public Toggl Focus API.
Note:
Authentication is implemented using a secure cookie stored in __Secure-accounts-session
info.title: Toggl Focus API

post.description:
Applies the proposed schedule changes and creates new tasks that were previously returned by /optimize.
The frontend holds the proposal and sends it back for confirmation.
Request accepts both changes (time block updates) and new_tasks (task creation) arrays.

get.description:
Fetches all TODO status tasks for the workspace, sends them to the scheduler service
for optimization, and returns proposed changes for tasks without existing time blocks.
Only unscheduled tasks (without start times) will be proposed for scheduling.
Response includes both proposed_changes (updates to existing blocks) and proposed_new_tasks (new tasks to create).

parameters.description: natural language instructions for the scheduler
parameters.name: instructions

post.description:
Tracks a PostHog event when the user rejects scheduler optimization suggestions.
This endpoint exists because the frontend cannot send PostHog events if the user hasn't accepted cookies.

put.description:
Submits positive or negative feedback for a scheduler optimization request.
At least one of kind or feedback must be provided.
put.summary: Submit user feedback for a scheduler request

post.description:
Validates user-provided scheduling instructions and returns a tier-based classification (A/B/C)
with parsed intent fields, inferred values, missing fields, and improvement suggestions.
Returns a Tier C fallback if the validation service is temporarily unavailable.

parameters.description: Instructions to validate. Optionally includes task_id/project_id from mentions.
parameters.name: body

get.description:
Searches time entries by description, tasks by name, and projects by name.
Returns grouped results for autocomplete use cases. Time entries include results
where the linked task has been soft-deleted (task fields will be null).
Time entries are always scoped to the authenticated user.
Deduplication of tasks across the time_entries and tasks groups is best-effort:
only task IDs from the returned time entries (limited by per_group) are excluded.
Keywords longer than 255 characters are truncated. Min 3 characters required.
get.summary: Unified search across time entries, tasks, and projects

post.description: Generates fake data for the specified workspace

get.description: Returns the current onboarding answers for the authenticated user in the workspace. Returns an empty default if no record exists.

patch.description: Creates or updates onboarding answers with merge semantics. If no record exists, one is created. On subsequent calls, provided fields are merged into the existing record. Omitted fields are unchanged. Setting an answer to null clears it. Setting completed to true sets completed_at.

get.description:
Returns the complete list of permissions for the current user in the specified workspace.
This combines unconditional permissions from the user's role with conditional permissions based on workspace settings.
get.summary: Get current user's permissions in workspace

403.description: User does not have access to workspace

parameters.description: Filter saved views by name (partial match)
parameters.name: name

patch.description: Update an existing saved view for the authenticated user

get.description: Returns permission settings for workspace roles. Settings control which roles have access to specific actions on clients, tags, and statuses.

403.description: User is not authorized to view workspace settings

put.description:
Updates permission settings for workspace roles. Creates settings if they don't exist (upsert operation).
The settings object should map role IDs to their permissions.
Note: Use role IDs as string keys (e.g., "15" for ws_admin, "16" for ws_manager, "17" for ws_user), not role codes.
Available permissions:
- Clients: create_client, rename_client, archive_client, delete_client
- Statuses: create_status, update_status, delete_status
- Tags: create_tags, update_tags, delete_tags
```json
{
"settings": {
"15": {
"create_client": true,
"rename_client": true,
"archive_client": true,
"delete_client": true,
"create_status": true,
"update_status": true,
"delete_status": true,
"create_tags": true,
"update_tags": true,
"delete_tags": true
},
"16": {
"create_client": true,
"rename_client": true,
"archive_client": false,
"delete_client": false,
"create_status": true,
"update_status": true,
"delete_status": false,
"create_tags": true,
"update_tags": true,
"delete_tags": false
},
"17": {
"create_client": false,
"rename_client": false,
"archive_client": false,
"delete_client": false,
"create_status": false,
"update_status": false,
"delete_status": false,
"create_tags": false,
"update_tags": false,
"delete_tags": false
}
}
}
```

active_users.description: ActiveUsers Number of users that have feature activated

enabled_for_all.description: Whether the feature is enabled for all users

enabled_for_user.description: Whether the feature is enabled for current user.

members.description: User data, and other membership details for adding members to the project.

milestones.description: The milestones to be created alongside the project.

rates.description: The rates to be created alongside the project.

resource_ids.description: The resource IDs/ghost IDs to be associated with the project.

private.description: Sets the generated tasks, and projects visibility

recipient_org_user_id.description: should come directly from accounts invite response `recipient_org_user_id` field.

recipient_user_account_id.description: should come directly from accounts invite response `recipient_user_account_id` field.

remind_tracking.description:
The following fields are deprecated but we need to keep them for backward compatibility with previous versions
of mobile apps

allocated_minutes.description: The summation of a user's time blocked minutes in the day

assigned_tasks_count.description: Number of tasks assigned to the user on the specified `Date`

estimated_minutes.description: The summation of a user's tasks' estimated minutes in the day

working_minutes.description: The user's per day working minutes set in their user preferences

computations.description: The computations that belong to the grouping entity

id.description: The ID of the entity that groups the computations

type.description: The type of the entity that groups the computations

masked.description:
Masked indicates the task belongs to a private project the user doesn't have access to.
When true, sensitive fields (Name, Client) are nulled.

weight.description:
The weight of a task in the timeline. This is not unique, and one or more
tasks may have the same weight as long as their dates do not conflict.

origin_product_id.description: The product that initially integrated the calendar.

id.description: The ID of one of the entities: projects/users determined by `Type`.

tasks.description: The tasks that belong to the group.

type.description:
`Type` determines what kind of group this is, and what table to look for
based on `ID`.

first_interaction.description:
FirstInteraction determines the settings update was the first user interaction
done. This is what is used to dispatch the Focus product sign-up event. This
is not a setting that can be managed by FE. This is purely an internal BE
setting.

has_reached_task_threshold.description: HasReachedTaskThreshold indicates the user has created 5+ tasks at some point.

plan_tour_dismissed.description: PlanTourDismissed indicates whether the user has dismissed the plan onboarding tour

start_week_on.description: StartWeekOn determines which day the week starts on: 0 for Sunday, 1 for Monday

tooltip_onboarding_dismissed.description: TooltipOnboardingDismissed indicates whether the user has dismissed the tooltip onboarding tour

welcome_modal_dismissed.description: WelcomeModalDismissed indicates whether the user has dismissed the welcome modal

degraded_reason.description: DegradedReason: timeout, unavailable, invalid_response, disabled, nil_result

time_block_total_count.description: TODO: Set as `required` after FE adjusts things on their end
